title: Intense heatwave returns, sultry weather troubles locals - The Times of India

full_text:
Times News Network
hot weather weather pic
TOI Lucknow Photo By V Sunil
Kanpur: Mercury rose once again on Sunday, bringing back intense heat and
discomfort for residents after a brief respite of one day. The combination
of soaring temperatures and high humidity left citizens perspiring
throughout the day.
The city recorded a maximum temperature of 37.4 degrees Celsius, nearly 3
degrees higher than Saturday’s 34.2 degrees Celsius. However, the minimum
temperature settled at 25.6 degrees Celsius, providing some relief during
the night hours.
According to weather data, maximum relative humidity was recorded at 70
percent, while north-easterly winds swept across the city at a speed of 4.6
km per hour. Though heatwave conditions were not felt on Sunday,
the high humidity level led to sultry weather conditions and excessive
sweating, troubling residents.
The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) has predicted that weather
conditions across all districts of the Kanpur division will remain mainly
clear and hot over the next 24 hours. Maximum temperatures in
Kanpur Nagar, Kanpur Dehat, Etawah, Farrukhabad, Kannauj and Auraiya are
likely to range between 39 degrees Celsius and 44 degrees Celsius. Intense
sunshine and severe heatwave conditions are expected to prevail during the
daytime.
In Kanpur Nagar, the sky is expected to remain mainly clear with bright
sunshine, though light clouds may appear during afternoon hours. The
maximum temperature may touch around 43 degrees Celsius. Hot
west-northwesterly winds blowing at speeds of 10 to 15 km per hour are
likely to intensify heatwave-like conditions in the city.
Meanwhile, Dr. S. Sunil Pandey, weather expert at CSA University, said that
due to atmospheric humidity, temperatures in the Kanpur division are
expected to rise gradually, resulting in continued sultry and humid
conditions.
